message:
🐛 Bake VITE_APP_VERSION into Docker images via build arg (#2337)

The Dockerfile never received APP_VERSION, so every Docker image
(dev and release) had VITE_APP_VERSION unset. vite.config.ts falls
back to getGitVersion() which returns '0.0.0' inside the container
(no .git directory). This made it impossible for users to see what
version they're running.

- Add ARG APP_VERSION to Dockerfile, wire it to VITE_APP_VERSION
- Pass APP_VERSION=main-<sha> in build-deploy.yml (dev builds)
- Pass APP_VERSION=<release-tag> in release.yml (release builds)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -18,7 +18,8 @@ FROM node:20-alpine AS frontend-builder
 
 WORKDIR /app
 
-# Build arg for commit hash
+# Build args for version and commit hash
+ARG APP_VERSION=0.0.0
 ARG COMMIT_HASH=unknown
 
 # Copy package files
@@ -28,7 +29,8 @@ RUN npm ci
 # Copy source
 COPY web/ ./
 
-# Build with commit hash
+# Build with version and commit hash baked into the JS bundle
+ENV VITE_APP_VERSION=${APP_VERSION}
 ENV VITE_COMMIT_HASH=${COMMIT_HASH}
 RUN npm run build
